message.content:
## Task: Audit attribute references in each RBS procedure section

Read `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBS-Specification.adoc` and for each of the 16 procedure sections below, extract every `{attribute_reference}` used in the narrative content (NOT in the mapping section, NOT in heading lines — just the body prose).

Then cross-reference against the RBSA mapping section to determine which references are:
- **OK**: Already in RBSA's mapping section (read `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc` lines 1-324 for the mapping section)
- **DEAD**: Uses `cmk_*`, `cfg_*`, `crg_*` vocabulary (dead, needs replacement with plain prose)
- **MISSING**: Uses a live prefix (`at_*`, `st_*`, `rbrn_*`, `rbrr_*`, `opss_*`, `mkr_*`, `scr_*`, `ops_*`, etc.) but the specific attribute isn't in RBSA's mapping section yet (needs to be added)

The 16 sections and their line ranges in RBS-Specification.adoc:

1. `opss_sentry_start` — lines 314-328
2. `mkr_network_create` — lines 329-386
3. `mkr_network_connect` — lines 387-429
4. `scr_security_config` — lines 430-467 (includes all 4 phases intro)
5. `scr_iptables_init` — lines 443-467
6. `scr_port_setup` — lines 468-511
7. `scr_access_setup` — lines 512-565
8. `scr_dns_step` — lines 566-662
9. `opbs_bottle_start` — lines 663-670
10. `mkr_bottle_cleanup` — lines 671-687
11. `mkr_bottle_launch` — lines 688-712
12. `opbr_bottle_run` — lines 713-720
13. `mkr_bottle_create` — lines 829-849
14. `mkr_command_exec` — lines 850-859
15. `ops_rbv_check` — lines 725-767
16. `ops_rbv_mirror` — lines 768-828

For each section, output a table like:

### Section N: attribute_name (RBSXX-filename.adoc)
| Attribute ref | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `{at_bottle_service}` | OK | in RBSA mapping |
| `{cmk_recipe_line_s}` | DEAD | cmk_* dead vocabulary |
| `{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}` | MISSING | rbrn_* but not in RBSA mapping |

At the end, provide a summary count per section: OK / DEAD / MISSING.

This is a READ-ONLY research task. Do not modify any files.

   178→[abstract]
   179→.Abstract
   180→The Recipe Bottle Makefile Service enables safe and effective use of foreign
   181→{st_container_s}
   182→at the
   183→{at_operator_p}
   184→{at_workstation}.
   185→
   186→== System Overview 
   187→
   188→=== Core Trust Challenge
   189→
   190→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   191→
   192→{st_container}-
   193→based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   194→
   195→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   196→
   197→* {st_container_s}
   198→provide powerful tool isolation
   199→* But they need controlled access
   200→to do useful work  
   201→* Standard
   202→{st_container}
   203→networking
   204→grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   205→
   206→=== The Bottle Pattern
   207→
   208→{at_rbm_system}
   209→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   210→{st_container}
   211→type here referred to as a
   212→{at_bottle_container}:
   213→
   214→. File Access Boundary
   215→* {at_bottle_container}
   216→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   217→{st_volume_mount_s}
   218→* No other access
   219→to
   220→{at_workstation}
   221→filesystem
   222→
   223→. Network Boundary  
   224→* {at_bottle_container}
   225→has no direct network access
   226→* All traffic
   227→flows through dedicated
   228→{at_sentry_container},
   229→a
   230→{st_container}
   231→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   232→* Only declared
   233→{st_port_map_s}
   234→and destinations
   235→allowed
   236→* Full logging
   237→of allowed and denied traffic
   238→
   239→Each
   240→{at_bottle_service}
   241→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   242→
   243→* One
   244→{at_sentry_container}
   245→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   246→* One
   247→{at_censer_container}
   248→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   249→* One
   250→{at_bottle_container}
   251→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   252→* Clear configuration
   253→in a single
   254→{at_nameplate}
   255→file
   256→
   257→The
   258→{at_censer_container}
   259→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   260→{at_sentry_container},
   261→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   262→{at_bottle_container}
   263→via `--net=container:censer`.
   264→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   265→{at_bottle_container}
   266→experiences only a functional path to its
   267→{at_sentry_container}
   268→gateway
   269→
   270→=== Security Properties
   271→
   272→The
   273→{at_rbm_system}
   274→maintains following essential guarantees:
   275→
   276→. File Access Control
   277→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   278→can access only explicitly configured
   279→{st_volume_mount_s}
   280→* No access to broader system resources
   281→
   282→. Network Isolation 
   283→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   284→{at_sentry_container}
   285→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   286→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   287→* Only explicitly configured
   288→{st_port_map_s}
   289→allow traffic between
   290→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network}
   291→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   292→have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
   293→{at_sentry_container_s}
   294→* Comprehensive traffic logging
   295→
   296→. Service Separation
   297→* Each
   298→{at_bottle_service}
   299→fully isolated
   300→* No cross-service communication
   301→* Independent security policies
   302→* Clean state separation
   303→
   304→// TODO fixup this language realized missing during pod namespace try
   305→. Service Access Control
   306→* External access to services only permitted when {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled
   307→* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
   308→* All service access must originate from outside the pod
   309→* Entry traffic flows: external → pod →
   310→{at_sentry_container} → {at_bottle_container}
   311→
   312→These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.
   313→
   314→== {opss_sentry_start}
   315→
   316→The
   317→{at_rbm_console}
   318→defines the
   319→{opss_sentry_start},
   320→a
   321→{cmk_external_rule}
   322→which is the precisely ordered sequence of
   323→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   324→needed to start a
   325→{at_sentry_container}
   326→that enforces network policy for a new
   327→{at_bottle_service}.
   328→
   329→=== {mkr_network_create}
   330→
   331→This sequence of
   332→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   333→within the
   334→{opss_sentry_start}
   335→establishes required networks for the
   336→{at_bottle_service}:
   337→
   338→. Remove Existing
   339→{at_transit_network}
   340→* Remove the
   341→{at_transit_network}
   342→named
   343→{at_transit_network_name},
   344→ignoring errors
   345→* This ensures a clean state before creating the new transit network
   346→
   347→. Create New
   348→{at_transit_network}
   349→* Use standard Podman network primitives to create a bridge network named
   350→{at_transit_network_name}
   351→* The IP address range or any other attributes remain consistent with the
   352→{rbrn_moniker}-uplink
   353→definition
   354→* IPv6 remains disabled, and external connectivity is enabled
   355→
   356→. Remove Existing
   357→{at_enclave_network}
   358→* Remove the
   359→{at_enclave_network}
   360→named
   361→{at_enclave_network_name},
   362→ignoring errors
   363→* This ensures a clean state before creating the new enclave network
   364→
   365→. Create New
   366→{at_enclave_network}
   367→* Use standard Podman network primitives to create an isolated bridge network named
   368→{at_enclave_network_name}
   369→* Configure the network with subnet
   370→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
   371→* Disable external connectivity to ensure isolation
   372→* Allow netavark to manage IP address assignment for containers on this network
   373→
   374→After this sequence completes, both networks exist in standard Podman configuration. The
   375→{at_sentry_container}
   376→will connect to both networks, while the
   377→{at_bottle_container}
   378→will connect only to the
   379→{at_enclave_network}.
   380→Before the
   381→{at_bottle_container}
   382→starts, an
   383→{at_ebpf_program}
   384→will be attached to its veth interface to ensure all traffic routes through the
   385→{at_sentry_container}.
   386→
   387→=== {mkr_network_connect}
   388→
   389→This sequence of
   390→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   391→within the
   392→{opss_sentry_start}
   393→establishes the secondary network interface connected to
   394→{at_enclave_network}:
   395→
   396→. Connect Container to Enclave Network
   397→* Attach the
   398→{at_sentry_container}
   399→to the
   400→{at_enclave_network}
   401→using standard Podman network connect
   402→* Verify successful network attachment
   403→* Validate interface naming sequence - eth0 must be the transit network interface
   404→* The enclave network interface must appear as eth1
   405→
   406→. Verify Network Interface Configuration
   407→* Execute within sentry container context
   408→* {mkc_interface_check}
   409→with interface `eth1` and timeout_s `5`
   410→** Exit on first success
   411→** Fail if timeout reached
   412→
   413→. Configure Sentry IP Address
   414→* Remove the auto-assigned IP address from eth1
   415→* Assign the permanent
   416→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
   417→address to eth1
   418→* This address will serve as the gateway for the
   419→{at_bottle_container}
   420→after eBPF frame rewriting
   421→
   422→After this sequence completes, the
   423→{at_sentry_container}
   424→has connectivity on both networks with the correct IP configuration, ready to receive traffic from the
   425→{at_bottle_container}
   426→once the
   427→{at_ebpf_program}
   428→is attached.
   429→
   430→=== {scr_security_config}
   431→
   432→One unified script executes these
   433→{cmk_script_line_s}
   434→in sequence, halting early if any
   435→{cmk_script_line}
   436→has a nonzero unix status:
   437→
   438→1. {scr_iptables_init} 
   439→2. {scr_port_setup}
   440→3. {scr_access_setup}
   441→4. {scr_dns_step}
   442→
   443→==== Phase 1: {scr_iptables_init} 
   444→
   445→Establishes foundational security state:
   446→
   447→. Base Chain Configuration:
   448→* Flush all rules from filter and nat tables
   449→* Set policy DROP on all base chains (INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT)
   450→* Accept loopback interface traffic unconditionally via first rule in INPUT chain
   451→
   452→. State Tracking Setup:
   453→* Insert RELATED,ESTABLISHED tracking as second rule in chains:
   454→** INPUT chain with "-m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT"
   455→** FORWARD chain with same state match
   456→** OUTPUT chain with same state match
   457→
   458→. RBM Chain Creation:
   459→* Create in filter table with default policy DROP:
   460→** RBM-INGRESS for input control
   461→** RBM-EGRESS for output control
   462→** RBM-FORWARD for forwarding control
   463→* Insert jumps as third rule in respective base chains:
   464→** INPUT jumps to RBM-INGRESS
   465→** OUTPUT jumps to RBM-EGRESS
   466→** FORWARD jumps to RBM-FORWARD
   467→
   468→==== Phase 2: {scr_port_setup}
   469→
   470→If
   471→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   472→is disabled, this sequence is skipped.
   473→If
   474→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   475→is enabled, this sequence configures service port mapping:
   476→
   477→. Socat Proxy Configuration:
   478→* Launch socat proxy process in background:
   479→** Listen on TCP port
   480→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
   481→** Forward connections to
   482→{at_bottle_container}
   483→IP (
   484→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   485→) on port
   486→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   487→** Enable connection forking and address reuse
   488→** Log output to `/var/log/socat-proxy.log`
   489→* Verify socat process started successfully
   490→
   491→. Filter Configuration:
   492→* Add rule to RBM-INGRESS accepting incoming traffic from bridge network:
   493→** Interface eth0
   494→** Protocol TCP
   495→** Destination port
   496→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
   497→* Add rule to RBM-EGRESS accepting outbound connections to bottle:
   498→** Interface eth1
   499→** Protocol TCP
   500→** Destination
   501→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   502→** Destination port
   503→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   504→* Add rule to RBM-INGRESS accepting return traffic from bottle:
   505→** Interface eth1
   506→** Protocol TCP
   507→** Source
   508→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   509→** Source port
   510→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   511→
   512→==== Phase 3: {scr_access_setup}
   513→
   514→If
   515→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   516→is disabled, this sequence:
   517→
   518→* Verifies DROP policy exists for all filter table chains
   519→* Removes NAT rules except those marked "RBM-PORT-FORWARD"
   520→* Adds explicit DROP rules for non-port traffic:
   521→** RBM-EGRESS: DROP all eth0 output
   522→** RBM-FORWARD: DROP all eth1 forward
   523→
   524→If
   525→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   526→is global or allowlist, this sequence:
   527→
   528→. Network Interface Configuration:
   529→* Enable IPv4 forwarding
   530→* Disable IPv6
   531→* Set rp_filter to strict mode (1) on all interfaces
   532→* Set route_localnet on eth0
   533→
   534→. NAT Configuration:
   535→* Add POSTROUTING on eth0:
   536→** Source from eth1 subnet
   537→** MASQUERADE to eth0 IP
   538→* Preserve rules marked "RBM-PORT-FORWARD"
   539→
   540→. DNS Server Access:
   541→* If
   542→{rbrr_dns_server}
   543→is configured:
   544→** Add rule to RBM-EGRESS accepting traffic to
   545→{rbrr_dns_server}
   546→** Add rule to RBM-FORWARD accepting traffic to
   547→{rbrr_dns_server}
   548→* These rules are independent of
   549→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   550→and
   551→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   552→
   553→. Filter Configuration:
   554→* If
   555→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is global:
   556→** Accept all output on eth0 in RBM-EGRESS
   557→** Accept all forward from eth1 in RBM-FORWARD
   558→* If
   559→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is allowlist:
   560→** For each CIDR in
   561→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}:
   562→*** Accept in RBM-EGRESS matching destination
   563→*** Accept in RBM-FORWARD matching destination
   564→* Maintain existing port rules
   565→
   566→==== Phase 4: {scr_dns_step}
   567→
   568→If
   569→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   570→is disabled, the
   571→{at_sentry_container}:
   572→
   573→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1
   574→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to eth0
   575→* Removes any existing DNS-related NAT rules
   576→
   577→If
   578→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   579→is global or allowlist, the
   580→{at_sentry_container}
   581→executes this sequence:
   582→
   583→. DNS Server Validation:
   584→+
   585→--
   586→* Verify
   587→{rbrr_dns_server}
   588→if specified:
   589→** TCP connect test to port 53 with 5 second timeout
   590→** UDP query for "." record with 6 second timeout
   591→* Fail if either test unsuccessful
   592→--
   593→
   594→. Dnsmasq Configuration:
   595→+
   596→--
   597→* Core Settings:
   598→** Interface binding limited strictly to
   599→{at_enclave_network}
   600→interface eth1
   601→** Use {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
   602→for listen-address
   603→** DNS service only - all DHCP functionality explicitly disabled
   604→** DNS resolution configuration isolated from host system settings
   605→** Clear existing upstream server configuration
   606→** Configure upstream DNS:
   607→*** Use configured upstream server when
   608→{rbrr_dns_server}
   609→specified
   610→*** Otherwise use host resolver via external network interface
   611→
   612→* Cache Settings:
   613→** Cache size: 1000 entries
   614→** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
   615→** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds
   616→
   617→* Query Control:
   618→** When
   619→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   620→is global:
   621→*** Accept all DNS queries from eth1
   622→*** Forward queries unmodified to upstream
   623→*** Log each query with timestamp and resolution
   624→*** Return all responses unmodified
   625→
   626→** When
   627→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   628→is allowlist:
   629→*** Load allowed domains from
   630→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
   631→*** Accept queries only for allowed domains
   632→*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries
   633→*** Configure dnsmasq logging directly with:
   634→**** log-queries=extra
   635→**** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
   636→**** log-dhcp
   637→**** Extra logging options in dnsmasq.conf:
   638→***** log-debug (for detailed query info)
   639→***** log-async=20 (for better performance)
   640→--
   641→
   642→. Filter Rules:
   643→+
   644→--
   645→* Accept DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1:
   646→** UDP port 53
   647→** TCP port 53
   648→* Accept DNS forwarding in RBM-EGRESS to eth0:
   649→** UDP/TCP port 53 to
   650→{rbrr_dns_server}
   651→** Or to host resolver if no server specified
   652→--
   653→
   654→. NAT Configuration:
   655→+
   656→--
   657→* DNAT eth1 DNS to
   658→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}:53
   659→* SNAT outbound queries to eth0 IP
   660→* Preserve existing port forwarding rules
   661→--

   721→== VM Management Operations
   722→
   723→This section defines the operations for managing Podman VM images within the {at_rbm_system}.
   724→
   725→=== {ops_rbv_check}
   726→
   727→The {ops_rbv_check} verifies whether newer VM images are available upstream by comparing SHA digests. This operation uses a temporary {at_stash_machine} with crane installed to query registries without modifying configuration.
   728→
   729→==== {ops_rbv_check} Sequence
   730→
   731→. Create Temporary {at_stash_machine}
   732→* Initialize new Podman machine using name from
   733→{rbrr_stash_machine}
   734→* Start the machine and install crane from
   735→{rbrr_crane_tar_gz}
   736→* Verify crane installation completed successfully
   737→
   738→. Query Upstream Image
   739→* Use crane to query digest of
   740→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin}:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   741→* Store the returned SHA digest for comparison
   742→* Fail if query returns no valid digest
   743→
   744→. Verify Custom FQIN (if applicable)
   745→* If
   746→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}
   747→differs from standard origin:version pattern:
   748→** Query the FQIN directly using crane
   749→** Compare FQIN digest against upstream digest
   750→** Report any discrepancies as warnings
   751→
   752→. Cleanup Resources
   753→* Stop and remove the temporary
   754→{at_stash_machine}
   755→* Ensure no orphaned resources remain
   756→
   757→. Report Findings
   758→* Display current
   759→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   760→value
   761→* Display latest upstream SHA digest
   762→* Indicate required actions:
   763→** If SHA not set: request user to set
   764→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   765→** If SHA differs: notify update available
   766→** If SHA matches: confirm system is current
   767→
   768→=== {ops_rbv_mirror}
   769→
   770→The
   771→{ops_rbv_mirror}
   772→copies the chosen VM image from upstream to the user's GHCR, ensuring consistent deployment across environments.
   773→This operation validates preconditions, performs the copy, and verifies podman can access the mirrored image.
   774→
   775→==== {ops_rbv_mirror} Sequence
   776→
   777→. Validate Preconditions
   778→* If using standard origin (FQIN starts with origin):
   779→** Query current upstream SHA using crane
   780→** Compare with
   781→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   782→if defined
   783→** Fail if SHAs do not match, directing user to run
   784→{ops_rbv_check}
   785→first
   786→
   787→. Generate GHCR Target
   788→* Construct GHCR tag using registry naming pattern
   789→* Format:
   790→ghcr.io/{rbrr_registry_owner}/{rbrr_registry_name}/vmimage:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   791→
   792→. Check Existing Mirror
   793→* Query GHCR for existing image at target location
   794→* If found and SHA defined:
   795→** Verify existing image SHA matches
   796→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   797→** Exit successfully if match confirmed
   798→** Fail if SHAs differ
   799→
   800→. Copy Image to GHCR
   801→* Use crane to copy from
   802→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}
   803→to GHCR target
   804→* Verify copy completed without errors
   805→* Confirm transferred image integrity
   806→
   807→. Verify Copied Image
   808→* If
   809→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   810→is defined:
   811→** Query digest of newly copied GHCR image
   812→** Compare with expected SHA
   813→** Fail if verification shows mismatch
   814→
   815→. Validate Podman Access
   816→* Initialize test Podman machine using mirrored image
   817→* Capture initialization output through standard scrubbing process
   818→* Extract reported SHA from initialization
   819→* Compare with
   820→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   821→if defined
   822→* Remove test machine after validation
   823→
   824→. Report Success
   825→* Confirm mirror operation completed
   826→* Note image available at GHCR location
   827→* Verify podman can successfully use mirrored image
   828→

   860→== {cmk_script} Requirements
   861→
   862→The following requirements apply to all {cmk_script_s} within the {cmk_discipline}:
   863→
   864→=== Environment Variable Availability
   865→
   866→Each
   867→{at_startup_script}
   868→inherits and validates
   869→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
   870→through makefile export directives:
   871→
   872→. At script start, validate existence of all referenced variables using `: ${VARIABLE:?} && echo "VARIABLE = ${VARIABLE}"`
   873→. {cfg_base_regime}, {cfg_station_regime}, and {cfg_nameplate_regime}
   874→export their respective variables
   875→
   876→=== Environment and Structure Requirements
   877→- Each
   878→{cmk_script}
   879→begins with `#!/bin/sh`
   880→- Each
   881→{cmk_script}
   882→enables `set -e`
   883→- Each
   884→{cmk_script}
   885→validates required environment variables using `: ${VARIABLE:?}`
   886→- Each
   887→{cmk_script}
   888→echoes validated variable values
   889→
   890→=== Error Handling Requirements 
   891→- Every command that can fail is followed by `|| exit N` where N is a positive integer
   892→- Error handling applies within if/then/else blocks and loops
   893→- Common commands like echo use exit code 99 for failures
   894→* Exit Code Ranges and Meanings:
   895→** 10: IPTables failures
   896→** 20: Port forwarding configuration failures
   897→** 30: Network access configuration failures 
   898→** 31: Network routing/filtering setup failures
   899→** 32: CIDR rule configuration failures
   900→** 40: DNS server connectivity check failures
   901→** 41: DNS configuration file writing failures
   902→** 42: DNS service startup failures
   903→** 43: DNS rule configuration failures
   904→
   905→=== Control Flow Requirements
   906→- if/then/else statements are permitted for clear flow control
   907→- for/while loops are permitted for iteration
   908→- All commands within control structures maintain error handling
   909→- Break and continue are permitted within loops
   910→- No command substitution without error handling
   911→- No function definitions permitted
   912→- All logic is expressed through direct command sequences
   913→
   914→=== Operation Logging Requirements
   915→- Each echo uses double quotes
   916→- Each log line begins with "RBSpN: " where N is the phase number (1-5)
   917→- Echo statements replace inline comments
   918→- No shell comments (# or multi-line) permitted
   919→- Debug echo statements precede significant operations
   920→
   921→=== Example Conformant Script Structure
   922→
   923→```sh
   924→#!/bin/sh
   925→set -e
   926→
   927→: "${INPUT_DIR:?}"
   928→: "${OUTPUT_DIR:?}"
   929→
   930→echo "XYZp1: Processing files from ${INPUT_DIR} to ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
   931→
   932→if [ ! -d "${INPUT_DIR}" ]; then
   933→    echo "XYZp1: Input directory not found"
   934→    exit 1
   935→fi
   936→
   937→if [ ! -d "${OUTPUT_DIR}" ]; then
   938→    echo "XYZp1: Creating output directory"
   939→    mkdir -p "${OUTPUT_DIR}" || exit 2
   940→fi
   941→
   942→echo "XYZp1: Beginning file conversion"
   943→for src in "${INPUT_DIR}"/*.txt; do
   944→    if [ -f "${src}" ]; then
   945→        echo "XYZp1: Processing ${src}"
   946→        dest="${OUTPUT_DIR}/$(basename "${src}" .txt).upper"
   947→        tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' < "${src}" > "${dest}" || exit 3
   948→        wc -l "${dest}" > "${dest}.count" || exit 4
   949→    fi
   950→done
   951→
   952→echo "XYZp1: Processing complete"
   953→```

   955→== {cfg_base_regime} Definitions
   956→
   957→This section defines the
   958→{cfg_regime_variable_s}

   961→that defines the core configuration for the

   963→
   964→[[rbrr_dns_server]]
   965→{rbrr_dns_server}:: 
   966→An IPv4 address (e.g., "8.8.8.8") that specifies the upstream DNS resolver for all 
   967→{at_bottle_service_s}.
   968→When configured, the
   969→{at_sentry_container}
   970→forwards DNS queries to this address instead of the host resolver, enabling consistent name resolution across all
   971→{at_bottle_container_s}
   972→while maintaining the established security boundaries.
   973→If not specified, the system uses the host's resolver configuration.
   974→
   975→[[rbrr_nameplate_path]]
   976→{rbrr_nameplate_path}:: 
   977→A relative path (e.g., "recipes/nameplates") that specifies the directory containing all
   978→{at_nameplate}
   979→definitions for this
   980→{at_rbm_system}.
   981→This path is resolved relative to the
   982→{at_rbm_repo}
   983→root, enabling consistent access to configuration files across different installations.
   984→If not specified, the system uses "nameplates" in the repository root.
   985→
   986→[[rbrr_registry_server]]
   987→{rbrr_registry_server}:: 
   988→A fully-qualified domain name and optional port (e.g., "registry.example.com:5000") that specifies the container registry server for all
   989→{at_bottle_service_s}.
   990→The
   991→{at_rbm_system}
   992→retrieves all
   993→{st_published_image_s}
   994→from this registry, ensuring consistent image sourcing across the installation.
   995→If not specified, the system uses the default public container registry.
   996→
   997→[[rbrr_registry_owner]]
   998→{rbrr_registry_owner}::
   999→A GitHub username or organization name that specifies the owner of the container registry namespace where
  1000→{st_published_image_s}
  1001→are stored.
  1002→
  1003→[[rbrr_registry_name]]
  1004→{rbrr_registry_name}::
  1005→A GitHub repository name that defines the repository within the container registry and forms the complete registry path when combined with
  1006→{rbrr_registry_owner}.
  1007→
  1008→[[rbrr_github_pat_env]]
  1009→{rbrr_github_pat_env}::
  1010→A file path to an environment file containing GitHub credentials for API and
  1011→{at_container_registry}
  1012→authentication with required RBV_USERNAME and RBV_PAT variables.
  1013→
  1014→[[rbrr_build_architectures]]
  1015→{rbrr_build_architectures}::
  1016→A space-separated list of platform identifiers in os/arch format that specifies target architectures for
  1017→{st_container}
  1018→builds.
  1019→
  1020→[[rbrr_history_dir]]
  1021→{rbrr_history_dir}::
  1022→A relative path from the
  1023→{at_rbm_system}

  1026→[[rbrr_chosen_podman_version]]
  1027→{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}::

  1034→[[rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin]]
  1035→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}::

  1050→[[rbrr_operational_machine]]

  1058→== Architecture Term Definitions
  1059→
  1060→[[bottle_image]]
  1061→{at_bottle_image}::
  1062→A
  1063→{st_image}
  1064→that contains the service functionality intended to run within a
  1065→{at_bottle_container}.
  1066→This image is stored in the
  1067→{at_container_registry}
  1068→and is referenced by
  1069→{rbrn_bottle_repo_path} and {rbrn_bottle_image_tag}.
  1070→
  1071→[[bottle_service]]
  1072→{at_bottle_service}::
  1073→A complete service instance consisting of one
  1074→{at_sentry_container},
  1075→one
  1076→{at_censer_container},
  1077→and one
  1078→{at_bottle_container}
  1079→working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
  1080→
  1081→[[sessile_service]]
  1082→{at_sessile_service}::
  1083→A
  1084→{at_bottle_service}
  1085→that maintains a persistent
  1086→{at_bottle_container}
  1087→running continuously to provide long-term services like development tools or network services.
  1088→These services maintain state and provide stable network ports while enforcing all
  1089→{at_sentry_container}
  1090→security constraints.
  1091→
  1092→[[agile_service]]
  1093→{at_agile_service}::
  1094→A
  1095→{at_bottle_service}
  1096→that creates temporary
  1097→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1098→for single operations, immediately removing them upon completion. This pattern suits toolchain operations that primarily operate through
  1099→{st_volume_mount_s}
  1100→with minimal network requirements.
  1101→
  1102→[[workstation]]
  1103→{at_workstation}::
  1104→The local computing environment where the
  1105→{at_operator}
  1106→runs the
  1107→{at_rbm_system}.
  1108→This environment hosts all
  1109→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1110→and their associated networks while providing isolation from other system resources.
  1111→
  1112→[[enclave_network]]
  1113→{at_enclave_network}::
  1114→An isolated network connecting a
  1115→{at_bottle_container}
  1116→to its
  1117→{at_sentry_container},
  1118→using the
  1119→// TODO involve the new network namespace
  1120→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
  1121→for addressing.
  1122→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
  1123→{at_bottle_container}.
  1124→
  1125→[[transit_network]]
  1126→{at_transit_network}::
  1127→The network interface that connects a
  1128→{at_sentry_container}
  1129→to the
  1130→{at_workstation_p}
  1131→network resources.
  1132→This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.
  1133→
  1134→[[moniker]]
  1135→{at_moniker}::
  1136→A unique identifier specified by
  1137→{rbrn_moniker}
  1138→that distinguishes each
  1139→{at_bottle_service}
  1140→instance within the
  1141→{at_rbm_system}.
  1142→This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.
  1143→
  1144→[[nameplate]]
  1145→{at_nameplate}::
  1146→A configuration file stored in
  1147→{rbrr_nameplate_path}
  1148→that defines all aspects of a
  1149→{at_bottle_service},
  1150→including its images, network settings, and security policies.
  1151→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
  1152→{at_rbm_system}
  1153→can instantiate.
  1154→
  1155→[[transit_network_name]]
  1156→{at_transit_network_name}::
  1157→The unique network name formed by appending `-uplink` to the service's
  1158→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1159→The
  1160→{at_rbm_system}
  1161→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1162→`${rbrn_moniker}-uplink`
  1163→to identify the external-facing network for each
  1164→{at_sentry_container}
  1165→within the container runtime environment.
  1166→
  1167→[[enclave_network_name]]
  1168→{at_enclave_network_name}::
  1169→The unique network name formed by appending `-enclave` to the service's
  1170→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1171→The
  1172→{at_rbm_system}
  1173→uses this consistent naming pattern `${rbrn_moniker}-enclave` to identify the isolated internal network connecting each
  1174→{at_bottle_container}
  1175→to its
  1176→{at_sentry_container}.
  1177→
  1178→[[enclave_namespace_name]]
  1179→{at_enclave_namespace_name}::
  1180→The
  1181→// TODO enclave, transit, network, but then namespace: this all needs a term rebalance... but later
  1182→{at_bottle_service}
  1183→unique namespace name formed by appending `-namespace` to the service's
  1184→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1185→// TODO this definition is not precise, but I'm not currently smart enough to repair it
  1186→The
  1187→{at_rbm_system}
  1188→uses this consistent naming pattern `${rbrn_moniker}-namespace` to identify the isolated internal network namespace connecting each
  1189→{at_bottle_container}
  1190→to its
  1191→{at_sentry_container}.
  1192→
  1193→[[sentry_image]]
  1194→{at_sentry_image}::
  1195→A
  1196→{st_image}
  1197→that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
  1198→{at_sentry_container}.
  1199→This image is stored in the
  1200→{at_container_registry}
  1201→and is referenced by
  1202→{rbrn_sentry_repo_path} and {rbrn_sentry_image_tag}.
  1203→
  1204→[[sentry_container]]
  1205→{at_sentry_container}::
  1206→A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
  1207→{at_bottle_service}.
  1208→It connects to both
  1209→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network},
  1210→controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
  1211→
  1212→[[censer_container]]
  1213→{at_censer_container}::
  1214→A privileged container using a minimal image that establishes and configures the network namespace before the
  1215→{at_bottle_container}
  1216→starts.
  1217→It sets up routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
  1218→{at_sentry_container},
  1219→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
  1220→{at_bottle_container}
  1221→via `--net=container:censer`.
  1222→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet.
  1223→
  1224→[[bottle_container]]
  1225→{at_bottle_container}::
  1226→A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
  1227→{at_censer_container}.
  1228→It is connected only to the
  1229→{at_enclave_network}
  1230→and configured with specific
  1231→{st_volume_mount_s}.
  1232→All external communication flows through its associated
  1233→{at_sentry_container}.
  1234→
  1235→[[bottle_container_name]]
  1236→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1237→The 
  1238→{at_bottle_container}
  1239→formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1240→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1241→The
  1242→{at_rbm_system}
  1243→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1244→`${rbrn_moniker}-bottle`
  1245→to identify all
  1246→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1247→within the container runtime environment, enabling reliable service management and status tracking.
  1248→
  1249→[[sentry_container_name]]
  1250→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1251→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1252→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1253→The
  1254→{at_rbm_system}
  1255→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1256→`${rbrn_moniker}-sentry`
  1257→to identify all
  1258→{at_sentry_container_s}
  1259→within the container runtime environment, enabling reliable security policy enforcement and network management.
  1260→
  1261→[[rbm_console]]
  1262→{at_rbm_console}::
  1263→The primary
  1264→{cmk_makefile}
  1265→that implements the
  1266→{at_rbm_system_p}
  1267→operational rules. It provides
  1268→{cmk_external_rule_s} and {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1269→for managing
  1270→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1271→throughout their lifecycle.
  1272→
  1273→[[rbm_config_makefile]]
  1274→{at_rbm_config_makefile}::
  1275→A
  1276→{cmk_makefile}
  1277→that manages configuration settings for the
  1278→{at_rbm_system},
  1279→including
  1280→{at_rbm_secret_s}
  1281→and environment-specific parameters.
  1282→This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.
  1283→
  1284→[[rbm_system]]
  1285→{at_rbm_system}::
  1286→The complete Recipe Bottle Makefile system, consisting of the
  1287→{at_rbm_console}, {at_rbm_config_makefile}, {at_nameplate_s},
  1288→and associated tools.
  1289→This system enables secure deployment and management of containerized services.
  1290→
  1291→[[rbm_secret]]
  1292→{at_rbm_secret}::
  1293→A sensitive configuration value needed by the
  1294→{at_rbm_system},
  1295→such as
  1296→registry credentials.
  1297→These secrets are managed through the
  1298→{at_rbm_config_makefile}
  1299→and kept separate from version control.
  1300→
  1301→[[rbm_repo]]
  1302→{at_rbm_repo}::
  1303→The version control repository containing the
  1304→{at_rbm_system}
  1305→implementation, including the
  1306→{at_rbm_console}, {at_nameplate_s},
  1307→and documentation.
  1308→This repository serves as the authoritative source for system configuration.
  1309→
  1310→[[user_repo]]
  1311→{at_user_repo}::
  1312→A version control repository owned by an
  1313→{at_operator}.
  1314→The
  1315→{at_operator}
  1316→chooses how to instantiate the
  1317→{at_rbm_system}
  1318→there, though a git subtree is a good choice.
  1319→The
  1320→{at_operator}
  1321→configures the
  1322→{at_rbm_system}
  1323→by committing a
  1324→{cfg_base_regime}
  1325→makefile and then creates a set of
  1326→{at_nameplate_s}
  1327→compliant with the
  1328→{cfg_nameplate_regime}
  1329→to define
  1330→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1331→
  1332→[[container_registry]]
  1333→{at_container_registry}:: 
  1334→A service that stores and provides access to 
  1335→{st_published_image_s}.
  1336→
  1337→[[startup_script]]
  1338→{at_startup_script}::
  1339→A
  1340→{cmk_script}
  1341→that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
  1342→{at_bottle_service}.
  1343→These scripts follow strict requirements for execution environment, logging, and error handling.
  1344→
  1345→[[operator]]
  1346→{at_operator}::
  1347→An individual who maintains a local installation of the
  1348→{at_rbm_system}
  1349→on their
  1350→{at_workstation}
  1351→and holds RBRA Files that enable assumption of the system's defined roles.
  1352→
  1353→[[consumer]]
  1354→{rbtr_consumer}::
  1355→The role an
  1356→{at_operator}
  1357→assumes to run containerized services securely on their
  1358→{at_workstation}.
  1359→{rbtr_consumer_s}
  1360→control service configuration through
  1361→{at_nameplate_s}
  1362→and interact with services through controlled interfaces, distinct from roles that build and deploy the system.
  1363→
  1364→[[at_ebpf_program]]
  1365→{at_ebpf_program}::
  1366→An eBPF TC (Traffic Control) filter program attached to the
  1367→{at_bottle_container_p}
  1368→veth interface that intercepts and rewrites Ethernet frames at the packet level.
  1369→This program replaces the default gateway IP address assigned by netavark with the
  1370→{at_sentry_container_p}
  1371→IP address in all outbound frames, ensuring all
  1372→{at_bottle_container}
  1373→network traffic routes through its associated
  1374→{at_sentry_container}
  1375→without requiring network namespace manipulation or special container privileges.
  1376→
  1377→[[base_regime]]
  1378→{cfg_base_regime}::
  1379→A
  1380→{crg_regime}
  1381→that defines and enforces core system configuration through environment variables with the RBB_*
  1382→{crg_prefix}.
  1383→The
  1384→{at_rbm_repo}
  1385→contains exactly one Base Config makefile that exports these variables, establishing foundational configuration for a single
  1386→{at_rbm_system}
  1387→installation.
  1388→
  1389→[[station_regime]]
  1390→{cfg_station_regime}::
  1391→A
  1392→{crg_regime}
  1393→that defines and enforces deployment-specific configuration through environment variables with the RBS_*
  1394→{crg_prefix}.
  1395→Each
  1396→{at_operator}
  1397→maintains their own Station Config makefile outside version control, containing sensitive data like authentication tokens required for their specific installation.
  1398→
  1399→[[nameplate_regime]]
  1400→{cfg_nameplate_regime}::
  1401→A
  1402→{crg_regime}
  1403→that defines and enforces service configuration through environment variables with the RBRN_*
  1404→{crg_prefix}.
  1405→Each
  1406→{at_bottle_service}
  1407→has exactly one
  1408→{at_nameplate}
  1409→makefile that exports these variables, establishing that service's precise behavior and constraints.
  1410→
  1411→[[regime_variable]]
  1412→{cfg_regime_variable}::
  1413→A
  1414→{crg_variable}
  1415→exported by one of the three configuration regime makefiles, following that regime's
  1416→{crg_prefix}
  1417→(RBB_*, RBS_*, or RBRN_*).
  1418→Each
  1419→{at_startup_script}
  1420→validates required
  1421→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1422→using the ': ${VARIABLE:?}' pattern before use.
  1423→
  1424→[[stash_machine]]
  1425→{at_stash_machine}::
  1426→A temporary Podman virtual machine created with crane installed for performing registry queries and image operations.
  1427→These machines are removed after completing their specific tasks.
  1428→
  1429→[[operational_machine]]
  1430→{at_operational_machine}::
  1431→The persistent Podman virtual machine that hosts all bottle services and their associated containers in the RBM system.
  1432→
  1433→== {cfg_station_regime} Definitions
  1434→
  1435→This section defines the
  1436→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1437→which come from the
  1438→{cfg_station_regime}
  1439→that defines user specific configuration for the
  1440→{at_rbm_system}.
  1441→
  1442→This
  1443→{crg_regime}
  1444→provides settings that are specific to a particular local {at_rbm_repo} clone or checkout.
  1445→These settings typically contain sensitive data like authentication tokens that should not be committed to version control.
  1446→
  1447→== Nameplate Configuration Regime Definitions
  1448→
  1449→This section defines the
  1450→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1451→which come from the
  1452→{cfg_nameplate_regime}.
  1453→Each
  1454→{at_bottle_service}
  1455→has exactly one
  1456→{at_nameplate}
  1457→makefile that exports these variables, establishing that service's precise behavior and constraints.
  1458→
  1459→[[rbrn_moniker]]
  1460→{rbrn_moniker}::
  1461→A unique identifier that distinguishes this
  1462→{at_bottle_service}
  1463→from others in the
  1464→{at_rbm_system}.
  1465→
  1466→[[rbrn_description]]
  1467→{rbrn_description}::
  1468→A human-readable explanation of the
  1469→{at_bottle_service_p}
  1470→purpose and function.
  1471→
  1472→[[rbrn_sentry_moniker]]
  1473→{rbrn_sentry_moniker}::
  1474→The image moniker identifier for the
  1475→{at_sentry_image}
  1476→in the
  1477→{at_container_registry}.
  1478→
  1479→[[rbrn_bottle_moniker]]
  1480→{rbrn_bottle_moniker}::
  1481→The image moniker identifier for the
  1482→{at_bottle_image}
  1483→in the
  1484→{at_container_registry}.
  1485→
  1486→[[rbrn_sentry_image_tag]]
  1487→{rbrn_sentry_image_tag}::
  1488→The version identifier for the
  1489→{at_sentry_image}
  1490→to be deployed.
  1491→
  1492→[[rbrn_bottle_image_tag]]
  1493→{rbrn_bottle_image_tag}::
  1494→The version identifier for the
  1495→{at_bottle_image}
  1496→to be deployed.
  1497→
  1498→[[rbrn_entry_mode]]
  1499→{rbrn_entry_mode}::
  1500→The mode controlling whether the
  1501→{at_bottle_service}
  1502→exposes network ports through the
  1503→{at_sentry_container}.
  1504→When disabled, no entry ports are configured.
  1505→When enabled, entry ports are mapped through the
  1506→{at_sentry_container}.
  1507→
  1508→[[rbrn_entry_port_workstation]]
  1509→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}::
  1510→The network port number exposed on the
  1511→{at_transit_network}
  1512→when port service is enabled.
  1513→
  1514→[[rbrn_entry_port_enclave]]
  1515→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}::
  1516→The network port number used for communication between
  1517→{at_sentry_container}
  1518→and
  1519→{at_bottle_container}
  1520→across the
  1521→{at_enclave_network}.
  1522→
  1523→[[rbrn_uplink_dns_mode]]
  1524→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}::
  1525→The DNS resolution mode for bottle-initiated requests through the
  1526→{at_sentry_container}.
  1527→When disabled, the
  1528→{at_sentry_container} blocks all DNS queries from the
  1529→{at_bottle_container}.
  1530→When global, the
  1531→{at_sentry_container}
  1532→forwards all DNS queries unmodified to upstream.
  1533→When allowlist, the
  1534→{at_sentry_container}
  1535→allows resolution only for domains listed in
  1536→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
  1537→and returns NXDOMAIN for all other queries.
  1538→
  1539→[[rbrn_uplink_access_mode]]
  1540→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}::
  1541→The IP access mode for bottle-initiated connections through the
  1542→{at_transit_network}.
  1543→When disabled, all non-port traffic is blocked.
  1544→When global, unrestricted IP access is allowed.
  1545→When allowlist, only traffic to CIDRs listed in
  1546→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
  1547→is permitted.
  1548→
  1549→[[rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs]]
  1550→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}::
  1551→A space-separated list of CIDR ranges that define allowed outbound traffic through the
  1552→{at_transit_network}.
  1553→
  1554→[[rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains]]
  1555→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}::
  1556→A space-separated list of domain names that are allowed for DNS resolution through the
  1557→{at_sentry_container}.
  1558→
  1559→[[rbrn_enclave_netmask]]
  1560→{rbrn_enclave_netmask}::
  1561→The network mask width defining the size of address space available for the
  1562→{at_enclave_network}.
  1563→
  1564→[[rbrn_enclave_base_ip]]
  1565→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}::
  1566→The base IPv4 address that establishes the starting point of the
  1567→{at_enclave_network}
  1568→range.
  1569→
  1570→[[rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip]]
  1571→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}::
  1572→The permanent gateway IP address (typically .0.1) assigned to the
  1573→{at_sentry_container}
  1574→on the
  1575→{at_enclave_network}
  1576→after removing the initial auto-assigned address. This address serves as the gateway for all
  1577→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1578→on the
  1579→{at_enclave_network}.
  1580→
  1581→[[rbrn_volume_mounts]]
  1582→{rbrn_volume_mounts}::
  1583→A space-separated list of Podman volume mount arguments that define filesystem access for the
  1584→{at_bottle_container}.

  1586→== Console Makefile Elements
  1587→
  1588→[[cmk_console_discipline]]
  1589→{cmk_discipline}::
  1590→A systematic approach to automation that defines patterns for creating reliable, maintainable build systems through careful rule organization, strict error handling, and standardized operational sequencing. This discipline establishes conventions for multi-pass construction, naming patterns, and the relationships between makefiles, scripts, and configuration files.
  1591→
  1592→[[cmk_makefile]]
  1593→{cmk_makefile}::
  1594→A specific makefile implementation that follows the
  1595→{cmk_discipline},
  1596→expressing concrete automation through properly structured
  1597→{cmk_external_rule_s}, {cmk_internal_rule_s}, and {cmk_script_s}
  1598→while maintaining prescribed error handling and operational patterns.
  1599→
  1600→[[cmk_clause]]
  1601→{cmk_clause}::
  1602→A standardized sequence of commands or expressions within a
  1603→{cmk_makefile}
  1604→that provides consistent behavior across different rules and contexts while maintaining
  1605→{cmk_discipline}
  1606→patterns.
  1607→These clauses can be invoked directly, used with $(call), or incorporated into larger recipes.
  1608→
  1609→[[cmk_recipe_line]]
  1610→{cmk_recipe_line}::
  1611→A single command line within a
  1612→{cmk_external_rule} or {cmk_internal_rule}
  1613→that follows
  1614→{cmk_discipline}
  1615→conventions, expressing one concrete bash action.
  1616→
  1617→[[cmk_probe_line]]
  1618→{cmk_probe_line}::
  1619→A single makefile recipe line that executes a precise command within a container context.
  1620→Recipe lines form the verification backbone of the
  1621→{cmk_discipline},
  1622→ensuring proper state progression through operational sequences.
  1623→
  1624→[[cmk_internal_rule]]
  1625→{cmk_internal_rule}::
  1626→A makefile rule that coordinates implementation details within the
  1627→{cmk_discipline},
  1628→called by other
  1629→{cmk_external_rule_s} or {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1630→to perform specific operational sequences.
  1631→
  1632→[[cmk_external_rule]]
  1633→{cmk_external_rule}::
  1634→A makefile rule that provides primary interface points for
  1635→{at_operator}
  1636→use, typically invoked by shell scripts or direct user commands to initiate major system operations.
  1637→
  1638→[[cmk_script]]
  1639→{cmk_script}::
  1640→A bash script that exists as an integral part of a
  1641→{cmk_discipline}
  1642→installation, executed under control of
  1643→{cmk_external_rule_s} or {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1644→and sharing the makefile's operational context and constraints.
  1645→
  1646→[[cmk_script_line]]
  1647→{cmk_script_line}::
  1648→One or more command lines within a
  1649→{cmk_script}
  1650→that achieve a particular action in the script's operational sequence, following
  1651→{cmk_discipline}
  1652→conventions for error handling and status reporting.
  1653→Any failure in a script line results in a non-zero exit status.
  1654→
  1655→== Supporting Infrastructure Definitions
  1656→
  1657→[[container]]
  1658→{st_container}:: 
  1659→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
  1660→
  1661→[[dockerfile]]
  1662→{st_dockerfile}:: 
  1663→A specification file that defines how to build a 
  1664→{st_image}.
  1665→
  1666→[[image]] 
  1667→{st_image}:: 
  1668→A template used to create 
  1669→{st_container}
  1670→instances, containing the service's code, runtime, and dependencies.
  1671→
  1672→[[image_store]]
  1673→{st_image_store}::
  1674→The local storage location for
  1675→{st_image_s}
  1676→on the
  1677→{at_operator_p}
  1678→{at_workstation}.
  1679→
  1680→[[published_image]]
  1681→{st_published_image}:: 
  1682→A 
  1683→{st_image}
  1684→that has been uploaded to a 
  1685→{at_container_registry}.
